publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 39-40. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. I will write a line, tho' I am rather hurried, because tomorrow I cannot. From the enclosed, I expect the Hen here tomorrow.
Charlotte, I rejoice to say, is much better; gaining rapidly & satisfactorily.--
I forgot to tell you how very nice & remarkable a little thing her son is. You know
I am not very prone to admire little babies. But I never saw one so beautifully made, or so intelligent at that age.

I have been a little vexed with what I call a stupidity of the Hen's yesterday. She sent her man over all the way from Brampton, merely to bring me a note. It could have come perfectly alone, without such a chaperon, by a parcel. It has cost her much expense, & me much

trouble; for I could not get the man away from here. He wanted to stay & amuse himself; & declared there was no way of getting back before tomorrow. Whereas found he could have returned last night, or early this morning perfectly.
And Calliphronas actually had to go & take a place for him, before I could get him off today.
I have been sight-seeing; & also introduced to

I have been a little plagued with Tooth-ache again; quite in a new & uncomeatable place; & must go to Lauzelle about it the moment I leave the Hen. I fear too it is a very bad job; as indeed all my concerns of this sort usually are.
I am a little disappointed to have no letter from you today.
